;Drakes deck has passed through a severe evolution during Magic ages: now that Fire Drake is totally obsolete, the deck does not contain Red anymore, focusing in a better combination of Black & Blue spells and taking advantage fom Sea Drake's ability.

;Using only the Elementals from Unlimited and Time Elemental from The Dark, the Elementals, Classic deck proves to be well balanced and still effective even if most of the creatures are obsolete. As side note, remember to use Mana Drain both as defensive spell or to quickly get the mana to play one of the elementals.

;A very funny deck to play, using Homarids in combination with artifacts. Use Staff of Domination to untap Homarids after that they get shroud, and Distorting Lens with Homarid Shaman or Homarid Spawing Bed and Vedalken Shackles to sacrifice captured creatures into 1/1 tokens.

;This particular deck is made to make use of those nice golden Red & Blue cards. Niv-Mizzet, in fact, makes a great combo with Electrolyze, while Dracoplasm may be used with creatures captured with Ray of Command or Vedalken Shackles.

;Shifting Sky is used to change all permanents' color into White, to maximize the benefits from having only white-warded creatures. This version with Red is less effective than its Black counterpart since there are not implemented Red spells that directly interact with White, as Anarchy.
;Seight of Mind is used here with Magical Hack to modify the proprieties of Blue color-specific cards. Mainly used for Wrath of Marit lage, this deck also allows some weird combos.
;Seight of Mind and Magical Hack are used to modify the proprieties of Red color-specific cards: the main targets for S.o.M. are Magnetic Mountain, Spinal Villain or Wildfire Emissary.

;Seight of Mind and Magical Hack are used to modify the proprieties of White color-specific cards. The strenght of this deck is that some White cards are effective against multiple colors at once (as Paladin En-Vec or Greater Realm of Preservation) and, thus, are much more versatile than other colors.
